title: Suspicion Worklist
description: Deterministic, confidence-INDEPENDENT structural-risk worklist emitted by suspicion_scan.py (RFC #215 Phase 2, default path {durable_root}/suspicion_worklist.json). One entry per canon source_form that tripped >=1 structural risk class; the skeptic pass (skeptic_setup.py -> skeptic-pass-wf.template.js) reads ONLY this file to decide who to adversarially re-examine. producer_input_digest binds this worklist to the exact canon.json + manifest.json + canon_senses.json it was built from -- each file's own PATH STATE ('absent'/'regular'/'irregular', codex round 4) hashed alongside its bytes, so a purely state-only change (an absent sidecar replaced by a genuinely-empty regular file, or by a directory -- content b"" either way) moves the digest even though the raw bytes alone would not have -- plus resolved scan parameters + resolved LanguageConfig.raw_bytes + the producer code closure (suspicion_scan.py, occ_index.py, bootstrap_names.py, canon_senses.py, skeptic_constants.py) it was built from; skeptic_setup.py recomputes the identical digest (compute_producer_input_digest, the ONE shared algorithm) and REJECTS a stale worklist fail-closed, so a since-changed canon/manifest/senses-sidecar-or-its-state/config/scanner can never be silently reprocessed. Strict at every level (additionalProperties:false, full required[]) so a producer bug fails LOUD.

producer_input_digest.description: sha256 hex over 13 parts, EACH ONE (including the last) followed by a single NUL byte -- NUL-TERMINATED, never `NUL.join()` -- in this exact order: canon.json's own PATH STATE ('absent'/'regular'/'irregular') as ASCII bytes, then its raw content bytes; manifest.json's STATE then its raw content bytes; canon_senses.json's STATE then its raw content bytes (#243); resolved scan parameters, canonically serialized as `json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False, separators=(",", ":")).encode("utf-8")` (sorted keys, no whitespace after `,`/`:`, non-ASCII characters preserved literally rather than `\uXXXX`-escaped); resolved LanguageConfig.raw_bytes; then the raw bytes of EACH of these 5 files, one file PER PART -- five SEPARATE NUL-terminated parts, never concatenated together first into a single part -- in this exact order: suspicion_scan.py, then occ_index.py, then bootstrap_names.py, then canon_senses.py, then skeptic_constants.py (6 state/bytes parts + 1 params part + 1 raw_bytes part + 5 individually-terminated closure-file parts = 13; read fresh off disk, never cached). The state tag matters because an absent sidecar, a genuinely EMPTY (0-byte) regular file, and a directory/other irregular path all read as identical b"" content -- indistinguishable by raw bytes alone -- but now hash DIFFERENTLY via their own state (a schema-valid LOGICALLY-empty document, e.g. {"schema_version":1,"entries_by_source_form":{}}, was already distinguishable by its own non-empty bytes even before state-tagging; state only closes the absent/empty-regular/irregular collision, not that case). Each file's (state, bytes) pair is captured ONCE and reused for both this digest and that file's own downstream parse (e.g. canon_senses.json's own canon_senses.load_senses_from_snapshot()) -- never two independent reads of the same path that could describe two different on-disk versions. Re-verified by skeptic_setup.py via the ONE shared compute_producer_input_digest (never a second, independently-drifting copy).

entries.description: One entry per flagged source_form, deterministic order (see suspicion_scan.py). An empty array is the schema-valid 'nothing structurally suspicious' state.

source_form.description: The canon.json entry key (NFC surface form).

canonical_target_form.description: The entry's canonical_target_form (carried through for the merge-group / rescope context).

occurrence_refs.description: Every occurrence backing the flag. origin='block' refs (offsets into manifest.blocks[block].plain_text) are the CITABLE windows the skeptic feeds and evidences against; origin='verse_embedded' refs (offsets into the verse.store[].plain_text whose placeholder lives in `block`) are LABEL-ONLY -- counted for singleton/dispersion/all_citation precision but NOT citable (evidence_verify authenticates against blocks{} only, so a verse citation coerces to insufficient_window).

block.description: For origin='block': the manifest block id. For origin='verse_embedded': the carrier parent_block id (a real blocks{} key per validate_extraction.py).

vid.description: verse.store vid; present ONLY for origin='verse_embedded' (identifies the embedded verse node whose plain_text the offsets index into).

group_key.description: normalize_form(canonical_target_form) -- present for merge_participant so the skeptic sees the whole over-merged group.

notes.description: Machine annotations (e.g. citation_classification_unavailable, verse_parent_unresolved, no_occurrences, near_pair_budget_truncated). Never a verdict.
